free_text: Cross Sections for Neutron Production from 6- and
tandem Van de Graaff accelerator at the Los Alamos
The detector was an NE-213 liquid scintillator
with 12 cm in diameter and effective thickness of 3
cm. It was in direct contact with the face of an
reaction. The target was a gas cell with an entrance
foil of 5.3 mg/cm2 of molybdenum and a beam stop of
0.05 cm of gold. Irradiation were performed at 0 deg.
Normalized net time spectra were converted to energy
spectra using first order relativistic approximation.

The uncertainty in incoming beam direction was 0.4 deg.

In the case of 10B at 6 MeV, neutrons from the first
excited state at 0.719 MeV (n,n1) could not be
confidently separated from the elastic scattering
peak at forward angles. The authors therefore used a
Legendre polynomial fit to the data of E.T.Sadowski
et al. combined with our back-angle data to generate
a correction to the sum over the elastic and first
of approximately 1.7 cm diameter and 4 cm height with

At 10 MeV, the authors summed the double differential
cross section over both states and used the
ENDF/B-VIII evaluated data for the first excited
state as a correction. An estimated uncertainty of
25% of the subtracted inelastic cross-section value
was combined quadratically with the experimental
statistical uncertainty to take into account the
uncertainty in the data used to make the correction.
